articles.title: LG's first RGB TV starts at $5000 and is available to pre-order today
articles.description:
LG has announced the pricing and availability of its Micro RGB evo, the company's first take on a TV display trend that kicked off in earnest at CES 2026. The LG Micro RGB evo is available to pre-order today starting at $5,000, and follows the recent release of the ultra-thin LG Wallpaper.
The Micro RGB evo represents the top of the line of a new class of display at LG that directly builds on the company's work with Mini LED technology. The new TV features LG's Micro RGB panel and its Alpha A11 AI processor, which runs the TV's webOS software, and perhaps more importantly, powers the "Micro RGB Engine" that controls the TVs individual LEDs. LG says the Micro RGB evo offers full gamut coverage across DCI-P3, BT.2020 and Adobe RGB, along with "enhanced contrast and refined detail" from the TV's over a thousand dimming zones.
While Micro RGB should offer better color representation than OLED, LG's OLED TVs still have their share of benefits, especially in things like contrast and dimming. Micro RGB panels are similar to the company's Mini LED ones, but rather than using all blue or white LEDs, the Micro RGB evo has individually controlled red, green and blue LEDs. The new style of display is also being explored by companies like TCL and Samsung, and at least for now, it's not as affordable as some QD-OLED or OLED TVs can be. 
The LG Micro RGB evo is available to pre-order today from LG's website in 75, 86 and 100-inch screen sizes. The TV starts at $5,000 for the smallest 75-inch model and goes up from there.
This article originally appeared on Engadget at https://www.engadget.com/home/home-theater/lgs-first-rgb-tv-starts-at-5000-and-is-available-to-pre-order-today-185159193.html?src=rss

articles.description: We wanted to show you what happens after the confetti falls. We checked in with some of our recent alumni, many of whom have sat down with us on Build Mode: The Founder Survival Guide, TechCrunch's podcast for founders at every stage.

articles.title: Google Cloud launches two new AI chips to compete with Nvidia
articles.description: Google's newest TPUs are faster and cheaper than the previous versions. But the company is still embracing Nvidia in its cloud — for now.

articles.title: Jadeja, Archer and Burger lead Royals to scrappy win against LSG
articles.description: RR broke a run of two defeats to climb to second place in the points table; LSG were down at ninth

articles.title: France confirms data breach at government agency that manages citizens’ IDs
articles.description: The French government agency that issues and manages national IDs, passports, and other documents, announced that hackers stole the personal information of an unspecified number of citizens.

articles.title: Apple fixes bug that cops used to extract deleted chat messages from iPhones
articles.description: The iPhone and iPad bug allowed law enforcement using forensic tools to read messages that had long been deleted by the Signal app.

articles.title: Iran war: Trump's pattern of setting unenforced deadlines
articles.description: This time around, the president did not provide a new deadline for how long the ceasefire extension will last.

articles.title: Parag: 'I've been going through a tough phase'
articles.description: "Right before the IPL, I had gone through something, and even now I'm going through something and everyone understands that"

articles.title: D4vd murder case: Medical examiner report released on teen's cause of death
articles.description: D4vd has been charged with first-degree murder in connection with the death of the teen, Celeste Rivas Hernandez, officials said.

topStories.title: New York Bans Government Employees from Insider Trading on Prediction Markets
topStories.description: A new executive order seen by WIRED prohibits New York state employees from using insider knowledge to enrich themselves with prediction market bets.

topStories.title: Inside Linda McMahon's effort to dismantle the Department of Education
topStories.description: A former pro-wrestling executive, McMahon is now the education secretary Trump tasked with abolishing the agency. New Yorker writer Zach Helfand explains how her WWE experience led her to this role.

topStories.title: WATCH:  Baby elephant makes debut on Earth Day
topStories.description: The elephant calf made her public debut as the first Asian elephant born at the National Zoo in more than 25 years.

topStories.title: Greetings from an Islamabad park, a peaceful vantage point in an uncertain world
topStories.description: The park, near the venue where inconclusive Iran-U.S. peace talks took place this month, provides respite to those who visit it.

topStories.title: WATCH:  Moments in cockpit after planes come dangerously close at JFK
topStories.description: Another close call between two planes at a busy U.S. airport. Anti-collision alarms sounded in both cockpits, forcing pilots to change course while attempting to land at New York’s JFK Airport.

topStories.title: Los Angeles is finally going underground
topStories.description: Los Angeles deserves its reputation as the quintessential car city—the rhythms of its 2,200 square miles are dictated by wide boulevards and concrete arcs of freeways. But it once had a world-class rail transit system, and for the last three decades, the city has been rebuilding a network of trolleys and subways. In May, a…

topStories.title: LIVE:  ABC News Live
topStories.description: 24/7 coverage of breaking news and live events

topStories.title: Palestinians Stream Back to Northern Gaza on Foot
topStories.description: Israel allowed displaced Gazans to begin crossing a military zone that bisects the enclave after a deadlock over hostage releases was broken.

topStories.title: Leading China Property Developer Reports Huge loss, in Sign of Widening Real-Estate Woes
topStories.description: Troubles at Vanke raise questions about the continued spread of the property crisis and whether the Chinese state will step in.

dailyNews.title: Freed Israeli Hostages Still Had Shrapnel in Their Bodies From Oct. 7 Attack
dailyNews.description: Some of the women were held alone for extended periods and spent eight months in tunnels, an Israeli medical official said.

dailyNews.title: Suspected Sabotage of Deep-Sea Cable Triggers First NATO-Led Response
dailyNews.description: The alliance mounted its first coordinated response to a suspected sabotage campaign against critical infrastructure after another cable was severed in the Baltic Sea.

dailyNews.title: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Enter Congo's Safe-Haven City
dailyNews.description: Residents of Goma reported gunfire and shelling after rebels overran Congolese troops. U.N. officials estimated that more than one million displaced people were now inside the city.

dailyNews.title: Cocaine-Funded Gangs Shake Colombia Years After Peace Pact
dailyNews.description: Attacks by powerful militias against civilians reflect the state’s inaction eight years after a peace accord removed a powerful rebel group from the field.

dailyNews.title: Border agents uncover RPG launcher, cache of rifles hidden in vehicle heading to Mexico
dailyNews.description: Officers discovered an RPG launcher and over 20 weapons concealed in a Lexus during a border inspection, preventing arms from reaching Mexican cartels.

dailyNews.title: Virginia voters approve new congressional map favoring Democrats, CBS News projects
dailyNews.description: Virginia voters on Tuesday approved a new congressional map that would give Democrats an advantage in 10 House districts, leaving just one safe Republican seat, CBS News projects.

dailyNews.title: Wife of Army soldier to be released after being arrested by ICE
dailyNews.description: Sgt. First Class Jose Serrano told CBS News he's been informed his wife will be released from an ICE detention center in El Paso.

dailyNews.title: Sources: NFL prospect Banks eyes June clearance
dailyNews.description: Florida's Caleb Banks, one of the top defensive tackles in this year's NFL draft, is expected to be fully cleared for football activities in early June after offseason foot surgery, sources tell ESPN.

dailyNews.title: Marine veteran allegedly gunned down by husband in attack outside home, police say
dailyNews.description: Marine veteran Ivy Unruh was allegedly shot by her estranged husband, Joshua Orlando, in an attack outside her Kansas home. Officials say she was separated from him.

dailyNews.title: With Scott's death, 5 members of current Congress have died in office
dailyNews.description: Democratic Rep. David Scott, who represented Georgia in the House for more than two decades, has become the fifth member of the 119th Congress to die in office.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Shopping Spree for Equity Stakes Nears $21B
dailyNews.description: Bloomberg's Scarlet Fu joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." The Trump administration’s $20.9 billion buying spree for equity stakes in private-sector companies is the biggest such push into strategic industries since the Second World War, according to research by the Council on Foreign Relations. (Source: Bloomberg)

dailyNews.title: Cowboys announce decision on George Pickens' contract status as he seeks major deal
dailyNews.description: The Dallas Cowboys said George Pickens will play under the franchise tag, with Stephen Jones ruling out long-term contract negotiations for the receiver.

topStories.title: Iran war: Trump's pattern of setting unenforced deadlines
topStories.description: This time around, the president did not provide a new deadline for how long the ceasefire extension will last.

(Source: Bloomberg)

topStories.title: US Could Own Up to 90% of Spirit
topStories.description: Bloomberg's Siddharth Philip joins Dani Burger and Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Deals." The Trump administration is nearing a rescue package for Spirit Airlines that could give the US government the option to own as much as 90% of the carrier once it emerges from bankruptcy, according to people familiar with the matter.  (Source: Bloomberg)

topStories.title: A 60-Day Deadline Could Pressure Trump on Ending the Iran War
topStories.description: A decades-old law allows the president to wage war without congressional approval for 60 days, then limits his options for continuing. President Trump may seek to get around it.

topStories.title: White House nears rescue deal for Spirit Airlines
topStories.description: White House nears rescue deal for Spirit Airlines

topStories.title: Bessent says 'many' U.S. allies have asked for currency swaps amid Iran war turbulence
topStories.description: The White House on Tuesday said the UAE had not requested a currency swap line.

topStories.title: Crypto mogul sues Trump family's World Liberty Financial, alleging fraud
topStories.description: Sun alleges that World Liberty Financial froze the digital tokens he had purchased, locking him out of assets worth as much as $1 billion.

topStories.title: Jeffries calls Trump 'dumbest person ever' to sit at 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue
topStories.description: Rep. Hakeem Jeffries calls Trump the dumbest person to ever sit at the White House, responding to the president calling him a low IQ person on Tuesday.

topStories.title: Trump claims Iran 'starving for cash,' 'collapsing financially' after extending ceasefire
topStories.description: President Donald Trump claims Iran is collapsing financially and losing $500 million a day as he extends the ceasefire and maintains a U.S. blockade of the country.

topStories.title: Kevin Warsh, Trump’s Fed Pick, Has Tough Task Shedding ‘Sock Puppet’ Label
topStories.description: Kevin M. Warsh, at his confirmation hearing to lead the Federal Reserve, sought to dispel doubts that he would yield to President Trump’s demand for lower rates.

topStories.title: Pace of N.I.H. Funding Slows Further in Trump’s Second Year
topStories.description: The agency has approved far fewer new grants than it did in years past. A renewed effort to screen for disfavored terms and a loss of personnel are contributing.

dailyNews.description: The agency has approved far fewer new grants than it did in years past. A renewed effort to screen for disfavored terms and a loss of personnel are contributing.

dailyNews.title: Iran attacks ships in Strait of Hormuz after Trump extends ceasefire
dailyNews.description: Iran renews attacks in the Strait of Hormuz after Trump says he's extending a ceasefire indefinitely, as thousands more U.S. forces head for the region.

dailyNews.title: Trump says 8 Iranian women protesters he asked to be released won't be executed
dailyNews.description: President Trump posted on social media Wednesday that eight women protesters who were going to be executed in Iran will no longer be killed. CBS News contributor Masih Alinejad shares her reaction.

dailyNews.title: Trump in advanced talks with Spirit Airlines about possible bailout
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration is having advanced discussions with Spirit Airlines about a potential bailout loan of up to $500 million in exchange for warrants that would allow the government to take an ownership stake in the company, sources tell CBS news. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul has more.

dailyNews.title: Iran fires at ships in Strait of Hormuz despite ceasefire extension
dailyNews.description: Iran attacked at least 3 ships in the Strait of Hormuz, according to international news agencies, amid the indefinite ceasefire extension President Trump announced Tuesday. Robert Murrett, the deputy director for Syracuse University's Institute for Security Policy and Law, joins with more.

dailyNews.title: Fact-checking Trump's latest claims about the Supreme Court
dailyNews.description: President Trump said on social media that "Democrat justices stick together like glue" amid recent Supreme Court rulings. CBS News' Jan Crawford has the latest on his claims.

dailyNews.title: Trump administration, Spirit Airlines in advanced bailout talks, sources say
dailyNews.description: The negotiation comes after President Trump publicly said he wanted his administration to look at a rescue package for the budget carrier.

dailyNews.title: Iran displays apparent ballistic missile at Tehran rally
dailyNews.description: Iran’s Revolutionary Guard brought what appeared to be a ballistic missile to a rally in Tehran as possible talks with the U.S. in Pakistan broke down. President Trump said he was extending the ceasefire with Iran while awaiting a “unified proposal” from Tehran.

dailyNews.title: Spirit Airlines nears deal with Trump administration for $500 million rescue package
dailyNews.description: Spirit Airlines, a discount carrier, has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy twice in under a year. It's in talks with the Trump administration for a bailout.

dailyNews.title: Trump administration in advanced talks for Spirit Airlines rescue package, sources say
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration is preparing to rescue ailing budget carrier Spirit Airlines.

dailyNews.title: Trump recounts Tim Cook call to 'kiss my ass,' in stark look at White House dealmaking
dailyNews.description: Tech executives from Amazon, Apple, Google and Meta have dined with Trump and donated millions to his inauguration fund and the White House ballroom.

dailyNews.title: Strait of Hormuz remains basically closed as Iran seizes ships after Trump ceasefire extension
dailyNews.description: Ship traffic in the Strait of Hormuz is still way below normal levels before the war when more than 100 vessels crossed daily.

dailyNews.title: Iran says it has seized two ships in Strait of Hormuz after U.S. extends ceasefire
dailyNews.description: The attacks come shortly after President Donald Trump said the U.S. would extend the ceasefire with Iran to allow peace talks to continue.
